mission.title: milvus: [Bug]: The inserted primary key is different from the returned result
mission.description: [Bug]: The inserted primary key is different from the returned result. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify milvus troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your milvus installation:
```bash
pip show pymilvus | grep Version
```
Look for errors or warnings that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [Bug]: The inserted primary key is different from the…
steps.description: Fix #20415 / ```yaml ### Current Behavior when I insert data, the key number is less than 64 bits, but it will overflow Insert number: 9999999999999999 Max Int64: 9223372036854775807 ### Expected Behavior The inserted primary key number is equal with the returned res ```

steps.title: Confirm [Bug]: The inserted primary key is different from… is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
Test milvus to confirm the issue is resolved.
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary: See the fix PR for the community-verified solution: https://github.com/milvus-io/milvus/pull/20468
codeSnippets: ### Current Behavior when I insert data, the key number is less than 64 bits, but it will overflow Insert number: 9999999999999999 Max Int64: 9223372036854775807 ### Expected Behavior The inserted primary key number is equal with the returned result ### Steps To Reproduce

Same problem may also apply to other sdks, whose language uses float64 for number types.  eg: node sdk with typescript.
Can you help to test & prove it?
> Same problem may also apply to other sdks, whose language uses float64 for number types. eg: node sdk with typescript. Can you help to test & prove it?
